---
title: "Week 2 — Facilitator Notes"
subtitle: "Git and GitHub Fundamentals"
---

## Learning Objectives

Residents should leave able to clone, edit, commit, and push. They should understand the difference between Git (local) and GitHub (remote).

---

## Session Format

| Segment | Duration | Activity |
|---------|----------|----------|
| Concept | 15 min | Why version control matters |
| Setup | 15 min | Configure Git identity, verify SSH |
| Demo | 20 min | Clone, edit, commit, push |
| Exercise | 40 min | Fork, edit, pull request |

---

## Key Teaching Points

### Analogy: Save States in a Game

Git commits are like save states. You can always go back. GitHub is cloud backup of your saves.

### The Git Workflow (Visual)

Draw on whiteboard:

```
Working Directory
      ↓ git add
Staging Area (index)
      ↓ git commit
Local Repository
      ↓ git push
GitHub (remote)
```

### Why SSH vs HTTPS

- SSH: No password typing after initial setup. Required for this curriculum.
- HTTPS: Password every time (or personal access token).

If resident hasn't set up SSH from Week 1 setup guide, walk them through it now.

---

## Common Confusion Points

| Confusion | Clarification |
|-----------|---------------|
| "What's the difference between Git and GitHub?" | Git is the tool on your computer. GitHub is the website hosting your code. |
| "Why do I need to `add` then `commit`?" | Adding stages changes; commit saves them. Like reviewing photos before saving album. |
| "I committed but don't see it on GitHub" | You need `git push` to send to GitHub. |
| "What's a fork?" | Your personal copy of a repo. Changes to your fork don't affect original until pull request. |

---

## Live Demo Script

```bash
# Check identity
git config --global user.name
git config --global user.email

# Check SSH
ssh -T git@github.com

# Clone (use your own demo repo)
cd ~
mkdir git-demo
cd git-demo
git clone git@github.com:balinesthesia/mds-curriculum.git
cd mds-curriculum

# Show status
git status  # "nothing to commit, working tree clean"

# Make a change
echo "# Demo edit" >> README.md
git status  # shows modified

# Stage and commit
git add README.md
git status  # shows staged
git commit -m "Demo: add header to README"
git log --oneline -3

# Don't actually push in demo — residents do this in exercise
```

---

## Exercise Walkthrough

**Fork and PR exercise:**

1. **Fork:** Show on GitHub UI. Point out "forked from balinesthesia/mds-curriculum" at top.

2. **Clone their fork:** 
   ```bash
   git clone git@github.com:USERNAME/mds-curriculum.git my-fork
   cd my-fork
   ```

3. **Branch:** Explain branches as parallel universes.
   ```bash
   git checkout -b my-first-edit
   ```

4. **Edit:** Open README.md, add comment with name.

5. **Commit:** Standard add/commit.

6. **Push:** 
   ```bash
   git push origin my-first-edit
   ```

7. **Pull Request:** Show GitHub UI. "Compare & pull request" button appears.

**Common failure:** Pushing to `origin` when they should push to their fork. Check remote:
```bash
git remote -v
```
Should show `git@github.com:USERNAME/...` not `git@github.com:balinesthesia/...`

---

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| "Permission denied (publickey)" | SSH key not added. Run `ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519` |
| "fatal: not a git repository" | Not in a git folder. Check `pwd` |
| "Merge conflict" (rare in intro) | Abort: `git merge --abort`; redo carefully |
| Pushed to wrong repo | Show how to check remotes; re-clone if needed |

---

## Assessment

Verify each resident can:

- [ ] Configure Git identity
- [ ] Clone their fork of mds-curriculum
- [ ] Make an edit, commit, and push
- [ ] Create a pull request

---

## Preparation Checklist

- [ ] Created demo fork for live demo
- [ ] Prepared SSH troubleshooting handout
- [ ] Identified residents who missed Week 1 setup
- [ ] Ready to pair-program with struggling residents
